Patch Set 4: Looks good to me, approved

Seems reasonable as a fix to the current problem. 

For the long term, a better fix would be to have the "import" lprocfs file print out the obd_connect_data fields in addition to the flags, so that the maximum RPC size could be known directly instead of just checking for the version. If this was included into 2.4 it would be ok to assume 1MB max RPC size if this data was not available.

Patch Set 5: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

i'm setting -1 until we understand what's going on in echo.

Patch Set 5:

The goal of the 4MB RPC patch is to send 4MB IOs to the disk, so it makes sense to send the maximum number of pages to disk in a single request, rather than in separate requests.  If the userspace request is larger, it needs to be split.

If echo_client is splitting the userspace-requested IO because it is larger than PTLRPC_MAX_BRW_SIZE then it should be doing a preprw/commitrw around each IO request.

Patch Set 4: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 5: (1 inline comment)

if we need to re-use env context then lu_context_exit() must be called followed by lu_context_enter(). This will reset env context for another use.

commit.subject: LU-2598 tests: check nr_local in ofd_preprw()
commit.message:
LU-2598 tests: check nr_local in ofd_preprw()

This patch checks the number of bulk I/O RPC pages with
PTLRPC_MAX_BRW_PAGES in ofd_preprw() to avoid
LASSERT(iobuf->dr_npages < iobuf->dr_max_pages) occurring
while larger I/O size is specified.

The patch also fixes echo_client_prep_commit() to reuse
the env context so as to avoid LASSERT(info->fti_exp == NULL)
occurring while the bulk I/O size is larger than
PTLRPC_MAX_BRW_SIZE.

The patch also improves obdfilter-survey to handle the
case while interoprating with old server.

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes \
testlist=obdfilter-survey

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I4e93e4b7181caa696394bf5bf4c78d0d276fc7a1
